excerpt: Those whose faith is deficient (muqassirin) shall enter Hell
source_location_hint: Doctrinal exposition on faith and belief

excerpt: Ali (alayhis-salam) who was foremost among all Companions to believe, possesses superiority over all other believers
The angels are appointed to serve and worship Allah and never disobey His command
source_location_hint: Description of angelic nature and function

excerpt: Inner faith is the affirmation from the depths of the heart of the five principles confessed by the tongue
source_location_hint: Exposition of inner and outer dimensions of faith

excerpt: When the First Intellect confessed monotheism to the Originator Most High, it was granted knowledge of what was, is, and shall be
source_location_hint: Metaphysical exposition on emanation and knowledge

excerpt: Knowledge is the form that the soul creates by knowing a reality; when confessed, affirmed, and embedded through action, it becomes faith
Faith concerns the heart; Islam concerns the observance of rules governing marriage, inheritance, and protection of life and property
Those who occupied the highest stations derived their name from the Messenger of God; from these exalted presences, Islam itself emanated. Conversely, those of inferior rank gave rise to faith.
When one adds certain letters to the name Muhammad, the numerical value equals that of Islam; when those letters are added to the name Ali, the sum corresponds to the numerical value of faith.
The Messenger of God established the framework of Islam, while the Commander of the Faithful instituted the framework of faith.
